enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb 'to be' (am, is, are).

Example: She ______ my mother. → She is my mother.
Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb 'to have' (have / has).

questions.texte: I ______ one brother and two sisters.

enonce.texte:
Rewrite the sentences in the negative form.

Example: I have a sister. → I do not have a sister. OR I don't have a sister.

questions.reponse: She is not my mother. OR She isn't my mother.
I do not have a big family. OR I don't have a big family.
They are not my parents. OR They aren't my parents.
My father does not have a brother. OR My father doesn't have a brother.
I am not a student. OR I'm not a student.

enonce.texte:
Rewrite the sentences as questions.

Example: She is my sister. → Is she your sister?

questions.reponse: Does your mother have two children?

enonce.texte:
Read the text about Marie's family and answer the questions.

"Hello! My name is Marie. I am twelve years old. I am a student in Class 6B. I have a nuclear family. My father is a doctor. His name is Paul. My mother is a nurse. Her name is Sophie. I have one brother. His name is Jean. He is ten years old. I also have a sister. Her name is Claire. She is seven years old. My parents have three children: Jean, Claire, and me. We are a happy family."

questions.reponse: Marie's family members are: her father (Paul), her mother (Sophie), her brother (Jean), and her sister (Claire).

questions.texte: How many children do Marie's parents have?
questions.reponse: Marie's parents have three children: Jean, Claire, and Marie.

questions.texte: Analyze how Marie describes the relationships in her family. What verb does she use most?
questions.reponse: Marie uses the verb 'to be' (am, is, are) to describe relationships and the verb 'to have' (have, has) to express possession. For example: 'I am twelve years old' (to be), 'I have a nuclear family' (to have), 'My father is a doctor' (to be).

enonce.texte:
Write 5-6 sentences describing your nuclear family.

📝 Your description should include:

  • Your name, age, and class
  • The members of your nuclear family
  • The relationship between you and each member (use 'to be')
  • How many people are in your family (use 'to have')

questions.texte: Write your paragraph about your nuclear family.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

"My name is David. I am eleven years old. I am a student in Class 6A. I have a nuclear family. My father is a teacher. His name is Mr. Kamga. My mother is a shopkeeper. Her name is Mrs. Kamga. I have two brothers and one sister. They are students. My parents have four children. We are a happy family."

Evaluation Criteria:

  • Correct use of 'to be' (am, is, are)
  • Correct use of 'to have' (have, has)
  • Clear description of family relationships
  • Proper use of possessive adjectives (my, his, her)
  • Correct grammar and spelling

enonce.texte:
Interview a classmate about their nuclear family. Ask questions using 'to be' and 'to have'.

📋 Questions to ask:

  • What is your name?
  • How old are you?
  • Do you have brothers and sisters?
  • What are their names?
  • What do your parents do?
  • How many people are in your family?

questions.texte: Conduct the interview and write down the questions and answers.
questions.reponse:
Example Interview:

Student A (Interviewer): "What is your name?"

Student B: "My name is Fatou."

Student A: "How old are you?"

Student B: "I am twelve years old."

Student A: "Do you have brothers and sisters?"

Student B: "Yes, I have two brothers and one sister."

Student A: "What are their names?"

Student B: "My brothers are Adam and Youssouf. My sister is Aisha."

Student A: "What do your parents do?"

Student B: "My father is a farmer and my mother is a teacher."

Student A: "How many people are in your family?"

Student B: "There are six people in my family: my parents, my two brothers, my sister, and me."

Evaluation Criteria:

  • Correct use of interrogative forms
  • Correct use of 'to be' and 'to have'
  • Complete answers from the interviewee
  • Natural dialogue flow

enonce.texte:
Create a family tree of your nuclear family, then write a description.

📋 Instructions:

  • Draw a family tree showing your nuclear family
  • Label each person with their name and relationship to you
  • Write a description of your nuclear family based on the family tree
  • Use 'to be' and 'to have' correctly
images: A simple family tree diagram showing a nuclear family with parents and two children, with labels for each member

questions.texte: Draw your family tree and write your description.
questions.reponse:
Example Family Tree Description:

"Here is my family tree. My father is Mr. Mboumi. His name is Pierre. My mother is Mrs. Mboumi. Her name is Rose. I am their son. My name is Jean. I am ten years old. I have two sisters. Their names are Anne and Sophie. Anne is eight years old and Sophie is six years old. My parents have three children. They are Anne, Sophie, and me. We are a family of five."

Evaluation Criteria:

  • Clear family tree with correct labels
  • Correct use of 'to be' (am, is, are)
  • Correct use of 'to have' (have, has)
  • Correct use of possessive adjectives (my, his, her, our)
  • Clear description of relationships
  • Proper grammar and spelling
